title: Field deployment + beneficiary validation with secured partner
context: The project's headline outcome is 'deployed for a real beneficiary', not 'symbols merged'. This task closes the loop between symbol production and real-world use by conducting at least one confirmed deployment with the secured partner from research-001, and conducting beneficiary validation sessions across ≥2 contexts. The consent/ethics protocol from writing-001 must be followed. Findings from validation sessions feed back into design and cultural reviews for future symbols. This task can only proceed once research-001 has confirmed a partner channel.
objective: Conduct at least one confirmed real deployment of the symbol set with the secured partner, and run at least 2 beneficiary-validation sessions across ≥2 contexts; report findings and feed them back into the design/review process.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 1 confirmed real deployment: installed signage at a facility, a live AAC board incorporating the symbol set, or a distributed toolkit for a humanitarian/education partner — with the partner name, deployment context, and date recorded.

At least 2 beneficiary-validation sessions across ≥2 contexts (e.g. a low-literacy or AAC user, and facility staff); findings (usability observations, comprehension issues, cultural feedback) recorded in aggregate, de-identified form per the writing-001 protocol.
Consent and de-identification honored per the M0 comprehension-test and consent/ethics protocol (writing-001); no identifying participant data published.
Validation findings are documented and fed back into the design system, cultural-review checklist, and/or symbol review queues as actionable items.
Research output is published in docs/field-validation/ and licensed CC-BY-4.0.
output: docs/field-validation/deployment-report.md — a field-validation report documenting the confirmed deployment(s), beneficiary-validation session findings (aggregate, de-identified), and action items fed back into design/review; licensed CC-BY-4.0.
